heading: Purposes
label: 130102 Purposes
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)13010236:78b., , .June 17, 1959Pub. L. 86–47, § 373 Stat. 77In clause (5), the words “destroy, or impair” are substituted for “make for the destruction or impairment of” to eliminate unnecessary words.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)13010236:78b., , .June 17, 1959Pub. L. 86–47, § 373 Stat. 77In clause (5), the words “destroy, or impair” are substituted for “make for the destruction or impairment of” to eliminate unnecessary words.

children.text: to perpetuate the memory of the Grand Army of the Republic and of the men who saved the Union in 1861 to 1865;
to assist in every practicable way in preserving, and making available for research, documents and records pertaining to the Grand Army of the Republic and its members;
to cooperate in doing honor to all those who have served our country patriotically in any way;
to teach patriotism, the duties of citizenship, the true history of our country, and the love and honor of our flag;
to oppose every tendency or movement that would weaken loyalty to, destroy, or impair our constitutional Union; and
to inculcate and broadly sustain the American principles of representative government, equal rights, and impartial justice for all.
